description: The generic monitor loop specified by sl-monitoring-sl: a squeeze loop that audits a BASE loop's soft outputs (chiefly its accumulated skills) against the base loop's OWN upper and lower bounds, from an evidence base the base loop's actors do not hold. Dominant coherent-and-wrong: an over-generalized skill (right on the common case, silently wrong on a governed exception), or a mis-calibrated monitor that rubber-stamps (flags nothing) or saturates (flags everything). Gate S returns PASS / CARVE-OUT / REJECT. The base loop is run as a sub-agent (the sub_base bridge); only its soft outputs cross, never its rationale. The coordinator's editorial judgment — the one soft-side hole — closes at the human/external reviewer terminus. Generic: no domain, no instance (monitors: null; the bridge expands to a placeholder base-loop).

sources.name: Base loop's upper bound (the spec the skill must not contradict)
sources.notes: the monitor's U IS the base loop's U; the skill under test is judged against it, not read off it

sources.name: Base loop's executable oracle (differential comparator)
sources.notes: the monitor's L IS the base loop's L; trigger/validity tests run it and watch whether the verdict moves

sources.name: The base loop's consolidated skill (the soft output under test)
sources.notes: the learned heuristic the monitor decomposes and differential-tests; read as the artifact under test, never a bound

sources.name: The deciding actor's rationale for the skill
sources.notes: WHY the base actor learned the skill — stays in the sub-agent; the monitor must never read it (its forbidden move)

sources.name: Gate S verdict (PASS / CARVE-OUT / REJECT)
sources.notes: the per-skill verdict, with any bound-deferring carve-out cited to U

sources.name: External / human reviewer judgment
sources.notes: closes the coordinator's editorial soft side; no executable refuter

actors.name: Monitor-loop coordinator
actors.builds: the Gate S handshake, the verdict ledger, the flag-rate calibration, and the loop's SL-1.0 monitor-loop.sl.json

upper_bound.text: the base loop's U it adjudicates carve-outs against

lower_bound.text: the base oracle and the monitor's emitted verdicts (gate output it reconciles and flag-rate-calibrates)

forbidden_moves: rubber-stamp a carve-out it has not editorially judged

actors.name: Skill monitor (Gate S)
actors.builds: the per-skill verdict via the trigger test (ignore-signal) or validity test (defer-to-oracle)

upper_bound.text: the base loop's U the skill must not contradict on any reachable input

lower_bound.text: the base loop's executable oracle, used as a differential-testing comparator against the skill

forbidden_moves: read the producer's rationale for the skill
emit a silent fix instead of a verdict

actors.name: Bridge -> the observed base loop (run as a sub-agent)
actors.builds: the base loop collapsed to one row; returns only its soft outputs (the skill), never its rationale

upper_bound.text: the base loop's own bounds (see its document)

lower_bound.text: the base loop's machine verdict, returned

forbidden_moves: leak the base loop's rationale or implementation across the sub-agent boundary

actors.name: External / human reviewer (terminus)
actors.builds: the terminal judgment that closes the monitor coordinator's editorial soft side

upper_bound.text: the base loop's U: is the coordinator's carve-out genuinely a faithful reading of it?

lower_bound.text: no executable oracle — soft-vs-soft faithfulness of a carve-out has no hard refuter; this absence is why the reviewer is the terminus
lower_bound.absent_reason: the carve-out's faithfulness to U is not mechanically decidable; closed by a disjoint human/external reviewer, not a machine

forbidden_moves: claim the automation alone closed the soft side

disjointness.rationale: The monitor holds the base loop's U and L (a disjoint evidence base from the deciding actor, which holds only 'what I was caught doing'); the carve-out can only be derived by the actor holding U and L, never by the skill's producer. The base loop runs as a sub-agent so its rationale is physically absent from the monitor's context — the must_not_see on producer_rationale is real, not honorary. The coordinator's editorial judgment is the one soft-side hole and is closed by disjoint authority at the human reviewer terminus, not mechanically.
disjointness.load_bearing_barrier: the monitor and coordinator never see the producer's rationale (it never leaves the sub-agent); they judge the skill only against the base loop's U and L

catchability.blind_spot: the base loop emits a coherent-and-wrong skill — over-generalized, right on the common case, silently wrong on a governed exception
catchability.via: the trigger test (perturb the ignored signal, watch L's verdict move) or validity test

catchability.blind_spot: mis-calibration — rubber-stamping (flags nothing) or saturating (flags everything)
catchability.via: flag-rate calibration: a healthy monitor discriminates, leaving defer-to-oracle skills untouched and flagging only over-reaching ignore-signal skills

catchability.blind_spot: its own un-squeezed editorial judgment of whether a carve-out is faithful to the base loop's U
catchability.via: external/human review — the terminus that closes the soft side

disjointness.terminus: human_reviewer (external/human review closes the soft side; the automation alone does not)
